cityDNA.coreFantasy: Port Sargasso is a West-African gold-sail harbor city where cargo law, ancestor-mask authority, sacred shrines, public works, scholarship, and informal settlement all share one tide-sealed civic economy.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Port Sargasso should feel immediately distinct: West African civic life is organized around Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, and Slums / Informal Settlement, with West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails as the citywide symbol.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The city turns Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, and Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Ancestral Spirits into a working urban system rather than surface decoration.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Use Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Public Works Authority Hall, visible Cargo Yard Beacon, the central Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel, and the skyline-scale West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails as the core visual grammar.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A West African population whose institutions make daily life legible through section anchors: Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, Slums / Informal Settlement.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city is symbolized by West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails, Cargo Yard Beacon, Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel, and the repeating civic pattern of hall, landmark, monument, and nook in every section.

canonLocks: Port Sargasso is built for its West African population and should not be genericized into a neutral fantasy city.
The Wonder is West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails.
The Beacon is Cargo Yard Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five section spines are Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, Slums / Informal Settlement.
The tactical board uses 25 district slots, with District 13 as the seat-of-power center.
Exact sidecar names for civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks must be preserved verbatim.
provisionalCanon: Citizens likely treat Cargo Yard Beacon as the practical public signal point for citywide alerts and Conjunction response.
Daily politics likely moves through the named section institutions rather than through faction headquarters.
The city’s civic magic expresses through Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Ancestral Spirits when visible in public spaces.
rejectedReads: Port Sargasso as a generic fantasy settlement with the culture applied as ornament only.
Replacing exact section anchors with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, archive, or court when the sidecar provides a proper name.
Treating the Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power as interchangeable skyline props instead of civic machinery.
cautionFlags: Do not add unsupported per-district landmark names beyond the source-backed section anchors.
Keep all 25 districts, but derive their specific identity from the five section spines unless new district-specific sidecars exist.
Preserve the material language: Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen.
Preserve the terrain/water logic: Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast.

environment.environmentalLogic: Environment should make the city’s institutions readable: Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, Slums / Informal Settlement must feel embedded in Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, with water handled as Coast and public magic appearing as Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Ancestral Spirits.

skyline: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails dominates the far skyline and should be larger than every section landmark.
Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel anchors the central District 13 civic mass.
Cargo Yard Beacon is the section-1 signal landmark and must be readable from nearby districts.
Section skyline rhythm: Cargo Yard Beacon, Roadside Shrine, Survey Hall, Lecture Hall, Scrap Market.

civilization.people: The population is West African. Civic scenes should show them using the city’s architecture naturally: gathering near Cargo Yard Beacon, moving through Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, and treating the named anchors as practical institutions.
socialClasses: seat-of-power officials attached to Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel
section hall administrators and permit keepers
craft, market, transport, religious, or military guild households depending on district spine
street vendors, carriers, maintenance workers, and household service families
visitors and petitioners who navigate the city through public anchors
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel and expressed locally through the five section civic halls. Authority should be visible in courts, archives, public signals, permits, and named monuments rather than hidden abstraction.
civilization.religion: Religious and symbolic life should draw on Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Ancestral Spirits, the Wonder’s public symbolism, and whichever section anchors carry sacred or ceremonial roles.
customs: citizens use named halls and nooks as practical meeting points
public signals from the Beacon interrupt ordinary commerce and movement
section monuments preserve civic memory and local identity
visitors orient themselves by Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section major landmarks
formal petitions and judgments move toward District 13
bodyLanguage: citizens make room for official messengers near beacon approaches
locals gesture by landmark rather than by street name
public posture changes around courts, monuments, and halls
market or service workers keep one eye on signal points during tense periods
speechPatterns: street speech abbreviates section landmarks but not Wonder, Beacon, or Seat names
public announcements use practical civic language rather than poetic vagueness

streets.appearance: Street views should show Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, pedestrians using section halls, and sightlines back to Cargo Yard Beacon or Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel where appropriate.
streets.movement: Movement is organized by section spine: Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, Slums / Informal Settlement. Districts feed toward their civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook before converging on District 13 for authority scenes.
streets.lighting: Use civic blue illumination for official signal moments, practical lantern light for night scenes, and material-specific highlights on Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce terrain and water logic: Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context Coast.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings emphasize deliveries, hall openings, street maintenance, market preparation, and official notices; afternoons build toward hearings, training, guild work, or section-specific labor.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings compress around lanterns, food stalls, guarded thresholds, quieter civic errands, and beacon visibility against darker streets.

prestigious: Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel document ateliers
West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails ceremonial suppliers

housing.wealthy: Residences near Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel or high-status section courts use the best Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, private terraces, and controlled views of civic landmarks.
housing.middle: Households cluster above shops and along civic streets, sharing courtyards, balconies, workrooms, and access to section halls.
housing.poor: Lower-status housing is practical, crowded, and close to service yards or labor streets, but still uses local motifs and public landmarks for orientation.

wonder.name: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of West African identity made public and monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A one-per-city wonder: spectacular, unmistakable, and larger than every section landmark. It turns Port Sargasso's geography, race identity, material culture, and civic magic into one public symbol. Built for Port Sargasso's West African population, it uses Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast; magic expression: Civic Blue Illuminat
wonder.civicRole: A unifying civic symbol larger than section landmarks; it gives Port Sargasso its skyline identity and public myth.
storyHooks: a public ceremony at the Wonder changes city mood
maintenance access reveals hidden civic routes
the Wonder becomes the reference point during a beacon state

beacon.name: Cargo Yard Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: The public signal point where citywide warning becomes local action.
beacon.visualRole: A largest local landmark in this section, subordinate only to the city wonder and citadel. It belongs to Section 1 (Warehousing / Logistics) of Port Sargasso and should use White Limestone Civic Blocks, Market Arcades, Subterranean Vaults, stone and gold leaf materials, Lantern Lit, Gold Seals, Red Fabric Awnings, and the West African visual language. Design focus: stacked barrels, warehouses, loading bays, manifest
beacon.civicRole: The Section 1 signal landmark; citizens watch it for alerts, public notices, and Conjunction response.
storyHooks: a signal is interpreted before officials arrive
crowds block the approach
the beacon reveals which district reacts first

seatOfPower.name: Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Civic authority made visible through courts, archives, command platforms, and public legitimacy.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. It is not a faction HQ; it is the city's central civic authority. Built for Port Sargasso's West African population, it uses Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast; magic expression: Civic Blue Illumin
seatOfPower.civicRole: District 13 authority center for petitions, archives, judgments, and city-scale decisions; not a faction headquarters.
storyHooks: a sealed record contradicts public belief
a petition exposes citywide risk
command platforms become crowd-facing stages

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 / Warehousing / Logistics must preserve Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard Beacon, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, and Inventory Hall Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S2 / Shrine / Sacred Place must preserve Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, and Votive Grove Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S3 / Public Works Authority must preserve Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, and Permit Yard Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S4 / University / Lyceum must preserve University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, and Observatory Court Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.

S5 / Slums / Informal Settlement must preserve Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, and Patched Tenement Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Port Sargasso as a West African city using Limestone Blocks, Stone, Driftwood, Gold Leaf, Linen across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin.
Keep West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails as the skyline-scale Wonder, Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel as central D13 authority, and Cargo Yard Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Use section landmark distribution to avoid flattening the city: Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, Slums / Informal Settlement.
Water and terrain must follow source context: Coast; Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin.
requiredVisualElements: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails
elementsToAvoid: tourist West-African ornament without civic systems
invented district-specific landmarks not supported by sidecars
replacing proper anchor names with generic summaries
collapsing five section spines into one repeated street type
